title: Posterior Collapse as Automatic Spectral Pruning
abstract: We show that posterior collapse in $β$-VAEs implements automatic spectral pruning. A latent mode collapses if its contribution to reconstruction is below the cutoff set by $β$. Equilibrium solutions with different $β$ thus reveal a cascade of collapses as latent modes decouple from least to most useful.   We derive this as a consequence of the loss via a Landau stability analysis. We define a latent-rescaling-invariant order parameter that ranks active latent modes and whose collapse thresholds identify which effective variables to inspect first.   In the linear Gaussian case, the collapse spectrum, utility spectrum, and normalized PCA spectrum coincide, and each collapse follows a mean-field law. We test these predictions on the WorldClim dataset.
summary: This paper reframes the 'failure' of posterior collapse in VAEs as a useful feature that automatically prunes unimportant latent dimensions. By treating the regularization parameter as an effective temperature, the author shows that latent modes disappear in a predictable sequence based on their contribution to data reconstruction.
main_problem: Reinterpreting posterior collapse in beta-VAEs from a training failure to a functional mechanism for automatic spectral pruning and identifying the relationship between latent mode collapse and reconstruction utility.
main_result: In linear Gaussian VAEs, the collapse threshold, reconstruction utility, and PCA explained variance coincide, demonstrating that increasing regularization triggers a predictable cascade of mode collapses.
method: The author employs a Landau-style stability analysis to treat latent mode collapse as a phase transition and uses a beta-scan protocol to rank latent modes by their utility.
model_or_system: The study focuses on beta-Variational Autoencoders (beta-VAEs), specifically analyzing the linear Gaussian case and testing the framework on the WorldClim global climatology dataset.
key_observables: Posterior signal fraction (M^2), collapse threshold (T_k), reconstruction utility (delta D_k), signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), and the Jensen gap.
important_parameters: Regularization strength (beta), effective temperature (T), and decoder variance (sigma^2_dec).
main_assumptions: The primary theoretical derivation assumes a linear Gaussian VAE with a fixed isotropic decoder variance; the thermodynamic analogy is structural rather than a literal marginalization over microstates.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the collapse scan via posterior mean-square and signal fraction; Figure 2 compares truncated distortion to PCA; Figure 3 demonstrates the utility-threshold duality; Figure 4 shows posterior-scale and Jensen-gap diagnostics.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of posterior collapse, develops a Landau-style stability theory for mode inactivation, establishes the duality between collapse and utility in Gaussian models, and validates these findings via experiments on the WorldClim dataset.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 2: Truncated distortion and rank pruning.
Figure 3: Reconstruction utility vs collapse threshold.
Figure 4: WorldClim posterior-scale and Jensen-gap diagnostic for ranked latent modes.
